title: glaucium quadratifolium
intro: Glaucium quadratifolium, also known as the quadrifoliate poppy or glaucium, is a flowering plant that belongs to the Papaveraceae family. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to induce sweating and alleviate coughs. The plant contains compounds with anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds, and may also have a beneficial effect on respiratory health by reducing congestion and coughing. Herbal preparations of Glaucium quadratifolium can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, which can be used to support the immune system and promote overall well-being.
